accrueInterestView().details: Returns newTotalAssets, performanceFeeShares, managementFeeShares.The management fee is not bound to the interest, so it can make the share price go down.The management fees is taken even if the vault incurs some losses.Both fees are rounded down, so fee recipients could receive less than expected.The performance fee is taken on the "distributed interest" (which differs from the "real interest" because of the max rate).

approve(address,uint256).details: Returns success (always true because reverts on failure).

convertToAssets(uint256).details: Returns corresponding assets (rounded down).Takes into account performance and management fees.

convertToShares(uint256).details: Returns corresponding shares (rounded down).Takes into account performance and management fees.

forceDeallocate(address,bytes,uint256,ad.details: Returns shares withdrawn as penalty.When calling this function, a penalty is taken from onBehalf, in order to discourage allocation manipulations.The penalty is taken as a withdrawal for which assets are returned to the vault. In consequence, totalAssets is decreased normally along with totalSupply (the share price doesn't change except because of rounding errors), but the amount of assets actually controlled by the vault is not decreased.If a user has A assets in the vault, and that the vault is already fully illiquid, the optimal amount to force deallocate in order to exit the vault is min(liquidity_of_market, A / (1 + penalty)). This ensures that either the market is empty or that it leaves no shares nor liquidity after exiting.

increaseTimelock(bytes4,uint256).details: This function requires great caution because it can irreversibly disable submit for a selector.Existing pending operations submitted before increasing a timelock can still be executed at the initial executableAt.

maxDeposit(address).details: Gross underestimation because being revert-free cannot be guaranteed when calling the gate.

maxMint(address).details: Gross underestimation because being revert-free cannot be guaranteed when calling the gate.

maxRedeem(address).details: Gross underestimation because being revert-free cannot be guaranteed when calling the gate.

maxWithdraw(address).details: Gross underestimation because being revert-free cannot be guaranteed when calling the gate.

multicall(bytes[]).details: Useful for EOAs to batch admin calls.Does not return anything, because accounts who would use the return data would be contracts, which can do the multicall themselves.

permit(address,address,uint256,uint256,u.details: Signature malleability is not explicitly prevented but it is not a problem thanks to the nonce.

setAdapterRegistry(address).details: The no-op will revert if the registry now returns false for an already added adapter.

setLiquidityAdapterAndData(address,bytes.details: Whether newLiquidityAdapter is an adapter is checked in allocate/deallocate.

submit(bytes).details: Will revert if the timelock value is type(uint256).max or any value that overflows when added to the block timestamp.

transfer(address,uint256).details: Returns success (always true because reverts on failure).

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: Returns success (always true because reverts on failure).
